wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the BuddyAI project overview section focused on introducing the AI-powered mental health support platform for tertiary institution students. Explain the system's purpose as an early intervention tool for depression detection, its core objectives including conversational AI support, sentiment analysis, PHQ-9 assessment integration, and mood monitoring. Document the target audience, problem statement addressing mental health challenges in academic settings, and the system's role as a supportive rather than replacement tool for professional care. Include the technology stack overview covering Next.js frontend, Express backend, NLTK/VADER sentiment analysis, PostgreSQL database, and JWT authentication. Provide practical examples demonstrating the platform's capabilities and explain how it addresses the stigma, access, and awareness barriers that prevent students from seeking mental health support.

wiki_catalogs.name: Routing and Navigation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the routing and navigation section focused on Next.js Pages Router implementation. Explain the application layout structure, route configuration, and navigation patterns. Document the RootLayout component, metadata configuration, and font loading strategy. Cover the Navbar component implementation, navigation links, and responsive behavior. Include examples of route protection, dynamic routing patterns, and navigation state management. Address SEO optimization, meta tags configuration, and accessibility considerations for navigation elements. Provide practical examples of implementing new routes, handling route parameters, and managing navigation flow between different application sections.

wiki_catalogs.name: Introduction and Purpose
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the BuddyAI introduction and purpose section focused on explaining the platform's mission to address mental health challenges among tertiary institution students. Detail the problem statement highlighting the stigma, limited access, and awareness barriers that prevent students from seeking mental health support. Explain the system's role as an AI-powered early intervention tool designed to complement professional care rather than replace it. Document the core objectives including conversational AI support, automated PHQ-9 assessment integration, sentiment analysis for mood tracking, and personalized recommendation generation. Include statistics on mental health prevalence among students and the impact of early intervention. Provide concrete examples of how the platform addresses common scenarios such as anxiety, depression, stress management, and crisis situations. Emphasize the platform's commitment to accessibility, confidentiality, and student-centered care.

wiki_catalogs.name: Presentation Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the presentation layer architecture focused on the Next.js React application. Explain the frontend structure including routing system, component hierarchy, and state management patterns. Document the user interface components, navigation flow, and client-side authentication mechanisms. Detail the integration with backend APIs through the API library, form handling for assessments and mood tracking, and real-time chat interface implementation. Include styling architecture using Tailwind CSS, responsive design patterns, and accessibility considerations. Address client-side error handling, loading states, and user experience optimizations. Document the relationship between frontend components and backend services, including authentication token management and API communication patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the authentication system focused on user registration, login, and session management. Document the JWT-based authentication flow including token generation, validation, and refresh mechanisms. Explain password hashing using bcrypt, token verification middleware, and role-based access control. Cover the complete authentication endpoints (/api/auth/register, /api/auth/login, /api/auth/logout) with request/response schemas. Document user registration validation, login security measures, session handling, and token expiration policies. Include practical examples of client-side authentication integration, error handling for invalid credentials, and security best practices. Address password reset functionality, email verification processes, and account activation workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Entity Relationships
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive entity relationship documentation for the BuddyAI database schema focused on the relational model connecting Users, Conversations, Messages, Mood Entries, PHQ-9 Assessments, Recommendations, and Risk Alerts. Detail the foreign key relationships, referential integrity constraints, and cascade behaviors between entities. Document the one-to-many and many-to-one relationships using UML-style diagrams showing User → Conversation, Conversation → Message, User → MoodEntry, User → Phq9Assessment, and User → RiskAlert relationships. Explain the junction table patterns for bidirectional relationships and the rationale behind each relationship design. Include cardinality mappings, constraint enforcement, and data consistency mechanisms. Address relationship optimization strategies and query patterns that leverage these relationships.

wiki_catalogs.name: JWT Token Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for JWT token management implementation. Explain the token generation process including payload structure with user ID, email, and role claims. Document the token verification mechanism using secret keys and expiration handling. Cover token refresh strategies, token blacklist implementation for logout functionality, and secure storage recommendations. Include practical examples of token creation, validation, and renewal processes. Document error handling for expired or invalid tokens, token signing algorithms, and security considerations for token storage in browsers versus servers. Address token lifecycle management and best practices for secure token transmission.

wiki_catalogs.name: Questionnaire Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the PHQ-9 questionnaire management system. Document the 9-question clinical interview structure covering sleep, appetite, energy, concentration, sadness, worthlessness, suicidal thoughts, restlessness, and psychomotor changes. Explain question presentation logic, response validation (0-3 scoring scale), and assessment initiation workflow. Cover the questionnaire interface design, progress tracking, and user experience considerations. Include implementation details for question ordering, response collection, and form validation. Document the controller endpoints for assessment submission and validation rules. Provide examples of questionnaire administration, response handling, and data integrity checks.

wiki_catalogs.name: Conversation Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the conversation management subsystem. Explain the conversation lifecycle including creation, retrieval, and persistence mechanisms. Document the conversation model structure with fields for user association, timestamps, and status tracking. Cover the controller functions for creating new conversations, listing user conversations, retrieving conversation history, and managing conversation states. Include practical examples of conversation initialization, conversation ID generation, and conversation metadata handling. Address conversation ownership verification, access control patterns, and conversation cleanup procedures. Document error handling for invalid conversation IDs, unauthorized access attempts, and database connectivity issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Mood Entry Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the mood entry management system. Document the mood rating scale implementation (1-5 rating system), validation rules, and data entry workflows. Explain the mood entry creation process including automatic timestamping, user authentication requirements, and note-taking capabilities. Cover the frontend implementation for mood logging interface, rating selection components, and form validation. Include practical examples of mood entry creation, error handling for invalid ratings, and integration with the authentication middleware. Document the data structure for mood entries, storage requirements, and input sanitization processes. Address edge cases like duplicate entries, rating boundary conditions, and note length limitations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Text Processing Pipeline
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the text processing pipeline component. Explain the TextProcessor class implementation including text preprocessing steps such as tokenization using NLTK punkt_tab, stop-word removal using stopwords corpus, lowercasing operations, and punctuation normalization. Document the text cleaning workflow that handles special characters, numbers, and whitespace normalization. Cover the preprocessing logic that ensures robust text input for sentiment analysis, including fallback mechanisms when preprocessing removes all content. Include practical examples of text transformation workflows, preprocessing configuration options, and performance optimization techniques. Address edge cases in text processing and provide troubleshooting guidance for common preprocessing issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Risk Detection Algorithms
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the risk detection algorithms section. Explain the multi-factor risk assessment methodology combining PHQ-9 depression scores, sentiment analysis trends from chat conversations, and mood tracking patterns. Document the weighted scoring algorithm that evaluates severity thresholds, temporal trends, and behavioral indicators. Cover the machine learning components including VADER sentiment analysis integration, mood variability calculations, and clinical decision support rules. Include the risk scoring formula, threshold configurations, and algorithmic decision trees. Provide examples of risk calculation scenarios, score interpretations, and algorithm validation metrics. Address false positive reduction techniques, sensitivity adjustments, and integration with clinical guidelines for depression screening.

wiki_catalogs.name: Alert Creation and Triggering
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for alert creation and triggering mechanisms. Explain how alerts are automatically generated based on PHQ-9 assessment results, mood tracking patterns, and behavioral indicators. Document the risk scoring algorithms that determine alert thresholds, including severity levels and escalation criteria. Cover the alert generation workflow from data collection through automatic trigger activation. Include the integration points with assessment services, mood tracking systems, and risk evaluation logic. Provide examples of different scenarios that trigger alerts, such as high-risk assessment scores, declining mood trends, or concerning behavioral patterns. Address the alert timing mechanisms, batch processing capabilities, and real-time monitoring requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Student Dashboard
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the student dashboard system focused on personal mental health monitoring and self-care support. Explain the dashboard layout including recent assessment history display, mood trend visualization, personalized recommendations access, and resource navigation. Document the data aggregation logic for presenting meaningful insights from assessment results and mood tracking data. Cover the user interface components for displaying PHQ-9 scores, mood patterns, and AI-generated wellness suggestions. Include practical examples of dashboard navigation, interpreting assessment results, and accessing recommended coping strategies. Address the integration with other platform features like chat access, mood logging, and resource library. Document the privacy controls and data presentation preferences available to students.

wiki_catalogs.name: Unit Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for unit testing focused on individual component testing strategies. Document the unit testing framework setup using Vitest for TypeScript services and pytest for Python modules. Explain service layer testing including authentication service tests, assessment service tests, mood tracking tests, and risk assessment tests. Cover mock implementations for external dependencies, database stubs, and API integrations. Include test isolation techniques, dependency injection patterns, and test data management. Document assertion patterns, edge case testing, and error condition validation. Provide practical examples of testing asynchronous operations, error handling, and business logic validation. Address test organization, naming conventions, and maintainability best practices for unit tests.

wiki_catalogs.name: Containerization and Docker Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for containerization and Docker configuration focused on multi-service deployment architecture. Document the Docker Compose configuration including service definitions, networking setup, volume mounts, and environment variable management. Explain the individual Docker configurations for client, server, NLP service, and database containers with build contexts and dependency management. Cover container networking strategies, inter-service communication, and port mapping. Include practical examples of building and running containers locally, environment-specific configurations, and troubleshooting container startup issues. Document volume management for persistent data, cache storage, and log persistence. Address container resource allocation, memory limits, and CPU constraints. Provide guidance on container registry integration, image versioning, and deployment automation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for authentication endpoints focused on user registration, login, and profile management. Document POST /api/auth/register with email, password, and user details validation requirements. Detail POST /api/auth/login with credential verification and JWT token generation. Cover GET /api/auth/me with protected route access and user profile retrieval. Include request/response schemas, authentication header requirements using Bearer tokens, error response formats for invalid credentials, duplicate users, and validation failures. Provide practical examples with curl commands and JavaScript fetch implementations. Document security considerations including password hashing, token expiration, and session management. Address common integration patterns and troubleshooting authentication issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Navigation Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the navigation components, focusing primarily on the Navbar component implementation. Document the component's authentication state detection, conditional rendering based on user roles (student vs counselor), and responsive design patterns. Explain the routing logic for different navigation paths, logout functionality, and state management using React hooks. Include props interface, event handlers, styling classes, and integration with the authentication system. Provide examples of component usage, customization options for different user types, and troubleshooting common navigation issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Risk Monitoring Dashboard
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the risk monitoring dashboard focused on real-time student risk assessment and alert tracking. Explain the dashboard statistics display including total alerts, pending reviews, resolved cases, and risk level distributions. Document the filtering mechanisms for status (PENDING, REVIEWED, RESOLVED) and risk levels (LOW, MODERATE, HIGH, SEVERE). Cover the alert listing interface with student information display, risk badges, status indicators, and creation dates. Include practical examples of counselor workflows for prioritizing high-risk cases, tracking response times, and monitoring caseload effectiveness. Address the integration with the alert management system and how dashboard data feeds into intervention planning.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the getting started section focused on setting up the BuddyAI development environment. Document the complete installation process including prerequisite software requirements (Node.js, Python, Docker), environment variable configuration for both frontend and backend, database setup with PostgreSQL, and service dependencies including the NLP service. Provide step-by-step instructions for running the application locally using Docker Compose, accessing the web interface, and verifying system functionality. Include troubleshooting guidance for common setup issues, port configuration, and service connectivity problems. Document the development workflow including npm scripts, hot reloading setup, and local testing procedures. Address security considerations for development environments and provide guidance for configuring authentication and database connections.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the UI components section covering reusable React components and page implementations. Detail the Navbar component including authentication state display, navigation links, and responsive behavior. Document form components for login and registration including validation, error handling, and submission workflows. Explain chat interface components for conversation display, message input, and real-time updates. Cover dashboard components for student and counselor views, including data visualization and interactive elements. Include component props, state management, event handlers, and styling patterns. Provide usage examples, customization options, and integration guidelines for each component type.

wiki_catalogs.name: Key Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the BuddyAI key features section covering the platform's core functionality modules. Document the AI chat assistant with conversational support and sentiment analysis capabilities, explaining how the VADER sentiment analysis integrates with the chat interface to detect emotional states and trigger appropriate responses. Detail the PHQ-9 depression assessment system including questionnaire administration, automated scoring algorithms, risk classification mechanisms, and result interpretation. Explain the mood tracking system with daily logging capabilities, trend analysis visualization, and historical record maintenance. Document the personalized recommendation engine that generates coping strategies, wellness tips, and resource suggestions based on assessment results and mood patterns. Cover the counselor dashboard functionality including risk alert management, student monitoring tools, and administrative reporting features. Include the alert management system for high-risk cases and the administrative dashboard for system oversight. Provide practical examples demonstrating each feature's workflow and user interaction patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Services Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the backend services layer architecture focused on the Express.js server implementation. Thoroughly explain the server configuration, middleware stack including authentication and error handling, and service organization patterns. Document the layered architecture separating controllers, services, and data access layers. Include the middleware pipeline for request processing, authentication flow using JWT tokens, and error handling strategies. Detail the service composition pattern, dependency injection, and inter-service communication. Address scalability considerations, load balancing, and performance optimization techniques. Document the integration with Prisma ORM, database connection pooling, and transaction management. Explain the RESTful API design principles, request/response validation, and security middleware implementation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Chat and Conversation Services
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the chat and conversation services focused on AI-powered conversational support. Document the conversation management system including message creation, retrieval, and thread organization. Explain the integration with NLP sentiment analysis service using VADER and NLTK for emotional analysis. Cover the complete chat endpoints (/api/conversations, /api/messages) with request/response schemas and real-time communication patterns. Document message processing workflows, sentiment scoring algorithms, contextual response generation, and conversation history management. Include practical examples of chat client integration, message formatting, and NLP analysis results interpretation. Address conversation persistence, message encryption, and privacy considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed data model documentation for each table in the BuddyAI database schema focused on field definitions, data types, constraints, and business rules. Document the User model with authentication fields and role management, Conversation model with timestamp tracking, Message model with sentiment analysis fields, MoodEntry model with rating and note fields, Phq9Assessment model with JSON responses and severity levels, Recommendation model with risk-based content, and RiskAlert model with status tracking. For each model, specify primary keys, unique constraints, default values, validation rules, and business logic constraints. Include field-level documentation explaining the purpose and acceptable values for each attribute. Document enum types (Role, Sentiment, Sender, SeverityLevel, RiskLevel, AlertStatus) and their usage patterns throughout the schema.

wiki_catalogs.name: Password Security
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for password security implementation. Document the bcrypt hashing algorithm usage including salt generation, hash computation, and verification processes. Explain password strength requirements, validation rules, and security policies. Cover password reset mechanisms, secure password change procedures, and account lockout policies. Include practical examples of password hashing during user registration, password verification during login, and secure password update workflows. Document security considerations such as preventing timing attacks, rainbow table resistance, and secure password transmission. Address common vulnerabilities like weak passwords, password reuse, and secure credential storage practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Scoring Algorithm
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the PHQ-9 scoring algorithm implementation. Document the total score calculation methodology, ranging from 0-27 points with weighted responses. Explain the scoring thresholds for different severity classifications: minimal (0-4), mild (5-9), moderate (10-14), moderately severe (15-19), and severe (20-27). Detail the automated scoring logic, validation requirements, and result formatting. Include examples of score calculations for various response combinations, severity level determinations, and clinical interpretation guidelines. Document the service layer implementation, error handling for invalid responses, and integration with risk assessment workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Message Processing and Storage
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the message processing and storage system. Explain the message lifecycle from user input through NLP analysis to database persistence. Document the message model structure including text content, sentiment scores, timestamps, and user associations. Cover the message validation pipeline, preprocessing steps, and storage mechanisms. Detail the integration with the NLP service for sentiment analysis and the subsequent storage of processed results. Include examples of message validation rules, content sanitization, and error handling for malformed inputs. Address message ordering, pagination strategies, and efficient retrieval patterns for conversation history.

wiki_catalogs.name: Trend Analysis Algorithms
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the mood trend analysis algorithms. Document the statistical methods used for identifying mood patterns, seasonal variations, and improvement indicators. Explain the calculation of moving averages, trend lines, and correlation analysis with assessment results. Cover the implementation of seasonal decomposition, cyclic pattern recognition, and anomaly detection algorithms. Include mathematical formulas and algorithms for trend calculation, confidence intervals, and significance testing. Document the integration with external factors like assessment scores and life events. Provide examples of trend interpretation, pattern recognition, and automated insight generation. Address algorithmic bias mitigation, data smoothing techniques, and statistical validity measures.

wiki_catalogs.name: VADER Sentiment Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the VADER (Valence Aware Dictionary and sEntiment Reasoner) sentiment analysis integration. Explain the SentimentAnalyzer class implementation and VADER lexicon utilization for detecting positive, neutral, and negative emotional states. Document the compound score calculation methodology, individual sentiment score extraction (positive, negative, neutral), and threshold-based classification logic. Cover the sentiment intensity scaling, lexical feature detection, and context-aware sentiment scoring. Include practical examples of sentiment analysis workflows, score interpretation guidelines, and confidence assessment. Address VADER limitations, cultural considerations, and customization options for different text domains.

wiki_catalogs.name: Alert Management Workflow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the alert management workflow covering the complete lifecycle from risk detection to resolution. Document the alert creation process triggered by risk evaluation results, including automatic flagging mechanisms and manual review procedures. Explain the alert states (PENDING, REVIEWED, RESOLVED) and transition rules between states. Cover the counselor notification system including email alerts, dashboard notifications, and escalation protocols. Detail the alert prioritization system based on risk severity, frequency, and clinical urgency. Include the alert resolution process with follow-up actions, intervention tracking, and outcome documentation. Provide workflow examples showing typical alert scenarios, escalation paths, and resolution timelines. Address alert archiving, reporting, and quality assurance measures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Alert Status Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for alert status management and tracking workflows. Detail the three-tier status system: PENDING for initial review, REVIEWED for active intervention, and RESOLVED for closure. Document the status transition logic, approval workflows, and timeline tracking for each status phase. Explain the audit trail requirements, status change notifications, and reporting capabilities. Include the filtering and sorting mechanisms for managing large volumes of alerts. Cover the status update processes, validation rules, and error handling for invalid status transitions. Provide examples of typical status workflows, escalation triggers, and resolution criteria. Address the integration with counselor dashboards and administrative reporting systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Counselor Dashboard
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the counselor dashboard system focused on student monitoring and risk management capabilities. Explain the dashboard interface for viewing high-risk cases, reviewing student assessment histories, monitoring mood trends, and managing intervention workflows. Document the integration with the alert management system for tracking student risk levels and response times. Cover the student search and filtering capabilities, assessment review tools, and communication features with students. Include practical examples of counselor workflows including risk assessment interpretation, intervention planning, and progress tracking. Address the escalation procedures, documentation requirements, and quality assurance features available to counselors. Document the reporting tools for monitoring caseload effectiveness and student outcomes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Integration Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for integration testing focused on component interaction validation. Document the integration testing approach for database connectivity, middleware validation, and inter-service communication. Explain database integration testing using Prisma ORM, connection pooling, and transaction handling. Cover middleware testing including authentication middleware, error handling middleware, and request/response processing. Include NLP service integration testing with sentiment analysis workflows and external API communication. Document test environment setup, database seeding, and cleanup procedures. Provide examples of testing complex workflows, error propagation, and system boundary validation. Address performance considerations, timeout handling, and resource management in integration tests.

wiki_catalogs.name: Production Deployment and Scaling
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for production deployment and scaling focused on enterprise-grade deployment strategies. Document production environment setup including environment variables, secrets management, and configuration files. Explain deployment workflows for different environments (staging, production) with rollback procedures and blue-green deployments. Cover horizontal scaling strategies, load balancing configuration, and auto-scaling policies. Include cloud platform deployment options (AWS, Azure, GCP) with infrastructure-as-code examples. Document security hardening including SSL/TLS termination, firewall configuration, and network segmentation. Address performance optimization including database connection pooling, caching strategies, and CDN integration. Provide practical examples of deployment commands, monitoring setup, and capacity planning calculations. Include disaster recovery procedures, backup strategies, and maintenance windows scheduling.

wiki_catalogs.name: Chat API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for chat and conversation management endpoints focused on message handling and conversation lifecycle. Document conversation creation, message sending, retrieval, and management endpoints. Include POST /api/conversations for initiating new conversations, GET /api/conversations for listing user conversations, and message CRUD operations. Detail request/response schemas for conversation objects, message entities with sentiment analysis data, and pagination parameters. Cover authentication requirements, message validation rules, and NLP integration for sentiment analysis. Provide examples of conversation threading, message ordering, and real-time communication patterns. Document error handling for unauthorized access, invalid conversations, and message processing failures. Include rate limiting considerations and performance optimization for chat operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Forms
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for authentication form components covering both login and registration pages. Document form validation patterns, error handling mechanisms, and submission workflows. Explain the integration with authentication APIs, token management, and user state updates. Include form field configurations, validation rules, success/error feedback mechanisms, and redirect behaviors. Provide examples of form customization, accessibility features, and integration with the overall authentication flow. Address common authentication issues and their solutions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Alert Detail and Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the alert detail management system focused on comprehensive student case review and intervention tracking. Explain the individual alert view interface showing student demographics, risk assessment history, recent chat conversations, and mood tracking data. Document the alert status management workflow including changing status from PENDING to REVIEWED to RESOLVED. Cover the intervention documentation features, counselor notes, and progress tracking capabilities. Include practical examples of complete counselor workflows from initial alert review through intervention completion. Address the integration with assessment history, chat logs, and mood trends to provide comprehensive student context for decision-making.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the BuddyAI system architecture focused on the multi-tier intelligent system design. Describe the high-level architecture including Presentation Layer (web interface), Backend Application Layer (RESTful API), NLP & Analysis Layer (sentiment analysis), Decision Layer (recommendation engine), and Data Layer (PostgreSQL). Document component interactions showing data flow from user authentication through conversation management, sentiment analysis, PHQ-9 assessment processing, and recommendation generation. Explain the microservices architecture with independent NLP service, containerized deployment using Docker Compose, and inter-service communication patterns. Include infrastructure requirements, scalability considerations, and deployment topology. Address cross-cutting concerns like security (JWT authentication), monitoring, and disaster recovery. Document technology stack integration, third-party dependencies, and version compatibility requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Styling and Theming
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the styling and theming section focused on Tailwind CSS implementation and custom styling patterns. Explain the global CSS configuration, font loading strategy using Geist fonts, and utility-first approach. Document color schemes, typography scales, spacing systems, and responsive breakpoints. Cover component-specific styling patterns, CSS modules usage, and theme customization options. Include dark mode implementation, accessibility considerations for visual design, and cross-browser compatibility. Provide examples of custom component styling, animation implementations, and performance optimization techniques for CSS delivery.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the BuddyAI technology stack section documenting the complete technical architecture and component technologies. Detail the frontend stack built on Next.js 16 with React 19, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS for modern web application development. Explain the backend architecture using Express.js with TypeScript, Prisma ORM for database abstraction, and PostgreSQL for data persistence. Document the NLP service built with FastAPI and Python, integrating NLTK and VADER sentiment analysis libraries for emotional text processing. Cover the authentication system using JWT tokens with role-based access control for student and counselor accounts. Detail the containerization approach using Docker and Docker Compose for multi-service deployment. Include the development environment setup with Node.js, Python, and database configuration. Explain the build tools, testing frameworks (Vitest for backend, pytest for NLP service), and quality assurance tools. Document the deployment pipeline, monitoring solutions, and operational considerations. Provide version compatibility matrices and upgrade paths for each technology component.

wiki_catalogs.name: NLP Processing Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the NLP processing layer architecture focused on the standalone FastAPI sentiment analysis service. Explain the microservice architecture design, text preprocessing pipeline, and sentiment analysis workflow using NLTK and VADER algorithms. Document the service endpoints for text analysis, batch processing capabilities, and real-time sentiment scoring. Detail the integration patterns with the main backend service, request/response formatting, and error handling strategies. Include the model architecture for sentiment classification, confidence scoring, and threshold-based risk detection. Address performance optimization techniques, caching strategies, and scalability considerations for high-volume text processing. Document the service discovery mechanism, health checks, and monitoring integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Assessment Services
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the assessment services focused on PHQ-9 depression screening and risk classification. Document the complete assessment workflow including questionnaire administration, automated scoring algorithms, and risk categorization. Explain the assessment endpoints (/api/assessments, /api/risk) with detailed questionnaires, scoring logic, and interpretation guidelines. Cover the risk detection system including threshold calculations, alert generation, and counselor notification workflows. Document assessment history tracking, result archiving, and trend analysis capabilities. Include practical examples of assessment administration, score calculation algorithms, and risk level determination. Address assessment privacy, data retention policies, and integration with counseling workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Prisma ORM Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive Prisma ORM configuration documentation for the BuddyAI project focused on database client setup, connection management, and Prisma-specific configurations. Document the generator configuration for TypeScript client generation, datasource configuration for PostgreSQL connection, and environment variable management. Explain Prisma client initialization in the server application, connection pooling settings, and transaction management. Detail migration workflows including schema changes, database versioning, and rollback procedures. Document Prisma CLI commands for schema introspection, migration creation, and database synchronization. Include best practices for query optimization, batch operations, and error handling. Address Prisma Studio usage for database inspection and local development workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for role-based access control implementation. Explain the STUDENT and COUNSELLOR role definitions, permission matrices, and access control enforcement mechanisms. Document the requireRole middleware implementation, role validation processes, and route protection strategies. Include practical examples of protecting different API endpoints based on user roles, role-based menu visibility in the frontend, and dynamic permission checking. Cover role assignment during user registration, role updates, and privilege escalation controls. Address common access control scenarios such as counselor-only endpoints, student self-service areas, and administrative privileges. Document security considerations for role manipulation attempts and proper authorization checks throughout the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Risk Assessment Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the PHQ-9 risk assessment integration system. Document the clinical decision logic for moderate and severe risk categorization triggering counselor intervention. Explain the automatic risk alert generation, recommendation creation for severe cases, and integration with the risk alert management system. Cover the severity level mapping to risk categories, escalation procedures, and counselor notification workflows. Include examples of risk assessment triggers, alert generation processes, and clinical intervention protocols. Document the service layer coordination between assessment and risk management systems, data sharing mechanisms, and audit trail requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: NLP Integration and Sentiment Analysis
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the NLP integration and sentiment analysis system. Explain the VADER sentiment analysis implementation using NLTK for detecting emotional tone in user messages. Document the NLP service architecture including text preprocessing, sentiment scoring algorithms, and compound score interpretation. Cover the integration patterns between the main application and the standalone NLP service, including API communication protocols and error handling. Include practical examples of sentiment classification thresholds, emotional state detection, and response adaptation based on sentiment scores. Address performance optimization for batch processing, caching strategies for repeated analyses, and fallback mechanisms for NLP service failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Visualization and Reporting
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the mood tracking visualization and reporting system. Document the charting libraries and visualization components for displaying mood trends, seasonal patterns, and comparative analyses. Explain the interactive dashboard features including date range filtering, trend overlays, and statistical summaries. Cover the historical data presentation including calendar views, line graphs, and statistical summaries. Include the implementation of export functionality for reports, sharing capabilities, and data download options. Document the responsive design considerations, accessibility features, and mobile optimization. Provide examples of report generation, custom view configurations, and integration with clinical reporting systems. Address data privacy in visualizations, anonymization techniques, and secure sharing mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Endpoints and Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the NLP sentiment analysis service endpoints. Document the /analyze POST endpoint with request/response schemas, including AnalyzeRequest and AnalyzeResponse models. Explain the text analysis workflow, error handling strategies, and HTTP status code responses. Cover the /health GET endpoint for service monitoring and availability checking. Include comprehensive request/response examples, parameter validation rules, and integration patterns with the main BuddyAI backend service. Document CORS configuration, security considerations, and rate limiting approaches. Provide client integration examples showing how the backend service calls the NLP API, error propagation, and fallback mechanisms for NLP failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Counselor Dashboard Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for counselor dashboard integration focusing on the clinical interface for risk alert management. Document the counselor dashboard layout showing high-risk student listings, alert summaries, and case prioritization. Explain the individual alert viewing interface with student assessment history, recent chat transcripts, mood trends, and risk evaluation details. Cover the intervention planning tools including note-taking capabilities, follow-up scheduling, and resource recommendations. Detail the communication features allowing counselors to contact students, coordinate care, and document interventions. Include the reporting and analytics components showing risk trends, caseload statistics, and outcome metrics. Provide examples of dashboard navigation, alert review processes, and intervention documentation workflows. Address accessibility features, mobile responsiveness, and integration with external clinical systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Counselor Workflow and Interventions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for counselor workflows and intervention procedures within the alert management system. Document the counselor interface for reviewing alert details, accessing student profiles, and documenting interventions. Explain the step-by-step workflow from alert receipt through intervention completion, including evidence gathering, decision-making processes, and documentation requirements. Cover the integration with student assessment history, mood tracking data, and communication logs. Include the intervention planning tools, follow-up scheduling, and progress monitoring capabilities. Document the communication protocols with students, families, and campus resources. Provide examples of common intervention scenarios, documentation templates, and quality assurance measures. Address the training requirements, competency assessments, and performance metrics for counselor workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Administrative Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the administrative dashboard system focused on system monitoring, user management, and institutional oversight capabilities. Explain the administrative interface for viewing system-wide metrics, user enrollment statistics, platform utilization data, and performance indicators. Document the user management tools for adding/removing users, role assignments, and system access controls. Cover the system monitoring features including service health checks, database performance metrics, and NLP service status. Include practical examples of administrative workflows such as system reporting, capacity planning, and quality assurance monitoring. Address the integration with institutional requirements for mental health program evaluation, compliance reporting, and outcome measurement. Document the export capabilities for generating institutional summaries and research datasets.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for API testing focused on RESTful endpoint validation and request/response testing. Document the API testing strategy for all route endpoints including authentication, assessment, chat, mood tracking, risk management, alert handling, and dashboard functionalities. Explain HTTP method testing, status code validation, and response schema verification. Cover authentication testing with JWT tokens, role-based access control, and session validation. Include request payload validation, parameter testing, and error response handling. Document API contract testing, OpenAPI specification validation, and endpoint documentation verification. Provide examples of testing complex workflows, batch operations, and real-time communication endpoints. Address API performance testing, rate limiting validation, and security testing approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Monitoring and Maintenance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for monitoring and maintenance focused on system observability and operational excellence. Document monitoring setup including application metrics collection, health checks, and alerting mechanisms. Explain logging configuration for centralized log aggregation, structured logging, and log rotation strategies. Cover system monitoring including CPU, memory, disk, and network utilization tracking. Include database monitoring for query performance, connection pool status, and backup verification. Document application performance monitoring (APM) setup with tracing and profiling capabilities. Address maintenance procedures including patch management, security updates, and database maintenance tasks. Provide practical examples of setting up monitoring dashboards, configuring alerts, and troubleshooting performance issues. Include backup and restore procedures, data integrity verification, and disaster recovery testing. Document maintenance schedules, system updates, and change management processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Assessment API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for PHQ-9 assessment endpoints focused on mental health screening and evaluation. Document assessment creation, submission, and retrieval endpoints including question management, scoring algorithms, and risk classification. Detail POST /api/assessments for starting new assessments, PUT /api/assessments/:id for submitting answers, and GET /api/assessments/:id for retrieving results. Include request/response schemas for assessment questions, answer validation, calculated scores, and risk level classifications. Cover scoring logic, interpretation guidelines, and automated recommendation generation. Provide examples of assessment workflows, partial submissions, and result formatting. Document error handling for incomplete assessments, invalid responses, and scoring calculation failures. Include integration patterns with counseling workflows and risk alert triggering mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Chat Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the chat interface component covering conversation display, message input, and real-time interaction patterns. Document the chat history rendering, message formatting, user typing indicators, and AI response handling. Explain the integration with the backend chat API, websocket connections if applicable, and sentiment analysis display. Include component state management for message composition, attachment handling, and conversation persistence. Provide examples of chat customization, responsive design considerations, and troubleshooting connectivity issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Student Search and Filtering
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the student search and filtering capabilities within the counselor dashboard. Explain the search functionality for finding students by name, email, or ID, combined with filtering options for risk levels and alert statuses. Document the query parameter construction and API integration for dynamic filtering. Cover the user interface components including filter dropdowns, search input fields, and result display formatting. Include practical examples of common search scenarios such as finding high-risk students, locating students with pending alerts, or searching by specific criteria. Address performance considerations for large student populations and pagination strategies for optimal user experience.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Application
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the frontend application section focused on the Next.js React implementation. Explain the application structure including pages router, component architecture, routing configuration, and state management patterns. Document the UI components including Navbar, authentication forms, chat interface, assessment forms, and dashboard layouts. Cover styling strategy using Tailwind CSS and custom CSS modules, responsive design implementation, and accessibility considerations. Include API integration patterns, authentication flow implementation, and error handling strategies. Document the TypeScript configuration, build process, and deployment considerations. Provide practical examples of component usage, form handling, and real-time chat functionality. Address performance optimization techniques, code splitting strategies, and browser compatibility requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the API integration section covering HTTP client implementation and data fetching patterns. Detail the api.ts library including base URL configuration, request interceptors, response handling, and error management. Document authentication integration patterns, token management, and protected route handling. Explain real-time communication for chat functionality, WebSocket connections, and live data updates. Cover form submission workflows, data validation, and optimistic updates. Include examples of API mocking for development, error boundary implementation, and loading state management. Address security considerations, CORS handling, and performance optimization for API calls.

wiki_catalogs.name: Evaluation Metrics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the BuddyAI evaluation metrics section covering the platform's performance measurement and effectiveness indicators. Document the PHQ-9 assessment accuracy metrics including sensitivity, specificity, positive predictive value, and negative predictive value for depression detection. Explain the sentiment analysis evaluation using VADER scores with correlation analysis against clinical assessments and inter-rater reliability studies. Detail the user engagement metrics including daily active users, session duration, completion rates for assessments and mood tracking, and feature adoption rates. Cover the system performance indicators such as response times, API latency, database query performance, and concurrent user capacity. Document the qualitative feedback metrics from student participants, counselor evaluations, and institutional stakeholders. Include the effectiveness measures for early intervention outcomes, reduction in crisis incidents, and improvement in mental health literacy scores. Explain the data privacy and security metrics including encryption standards, access logs, audit trails, and compliance with educational data privacy regulations. Provide benchmark comparisons against traditional mental health support systems and cost-effectiveness analysis.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Layer Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the BuddyAI data layer architecture focused on the PostgreSQL database design and Prisma ORM implementation. Detail the entity-relationship model including User, Conversation, Message, MoodEntry, Phq9Assessment, Recommendation, and RiskAlert entities. Document field definitions, data types, primary/foreign key relationships, and database constraints. Explain the Prisma schema design, model relationships, and generated client API usage. Include data validation rules, business logic constraints, and referential integrity enforcement. Document database migration strategies, schema evolution patterns, and data seeding processes. Address data access patterns, query optimization techniques, and indexing strategies. Specify data lifecycle management, retention policies, and backup procedures. Include performance considerations, connection pooling, and transaction management patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Mood Tracking Services
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the mood tracking services focused on daily mood logging and trend analysis. Include practical examples of mood entry workflows, trend calculation methods, and personalized insights generation. Address mood privacy settings, data aggregation techniques, and integration with assessment results.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Management Policies

wiki_catalogs.name: Session Management

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for performance optimization strategies and configuration management in the NLP service. Address security coding practices, accessibility requirements, and internationalization considerations. Include guidelines for extending the system, adding new features, and maintaining backward compatibility.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# BuddyAI: AI-Powered Mental Health Support Platform

## Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
BuddyAI is a comprehensive mental health support platform designed specifically for students, providing AI-powered assistance, peer counseling, and personalized mental wellness tracking. The platform combines modern web technologies with advanced natural language processing to deliver accessible mental healthcare support.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Accessibility**: Provide 24/7 mental health support through AI chatbot technology
- **Personalization**: Offer tailored recommendations based on user assessments and mood tracking
- **Professional Integration**: Facilitate communication between students and certified counselors
- **Early Intervention**: Detect potential mental health risks through automated analysis
- **User-Friendly Experience**: Deliver intuitive interfaces for both students and counselors

### Target Audience
- **Primary**: Students seeking mental health support and resources
- **Secondary**: Mental health counselors and professionals
- **Tertiary**: Educational institutions supporting student wellbeing

## Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown

The BuddyAI platform follows a modern microservices architecture with three main components:

```mermaid
graph TB
    subgraph "Frontend Layer"
        A[Next.js Client] --> B[React Components]
        B --> C[Authentication]
        B --> D[Navigation]
        B --> E[Dashboard Views]
    end
    
    subgraph "Backend Services"
        F[Express Server] --> G[REST API Routes]
        G --> H[Controller Layer]
        H --> I[Service Layer]
        I --> J[Prisma ORM]
    end
    
    subgraph "NLP Processing"
        K[FastAPI Service] --> L[NLTK Analyzer]
        L --> M[VADER Sentiment]
    end
    
    subgraph "Data Layer"
        N[PostgreSQL Database]
        O[Prisma Client]
    end
    
    A --> F
    F --> K
    F --> N
    K --> N
```

### Design Patterns
- **MVC Pattern**: Clear separation between Models, Views, and Controllers
- **Layered Architecture**: Distinct layers for presentation, business logic, and data access
- **Microservices**: Independent services for different functional domains
- **Repository Pattern**: Abstracted data access through Prisma ORM
- **Middleware Pattern**: Centralized authentication and error handling

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User Interface
    participant S as Express Server
    participant N as NLP Service
    participant D as Database
    
    U->>S: API Request
    S->>N: Text Analysis Request
    N->>N: Sentiment Analysis
    N-->>S: Analysis Result
    S->>D: Database Operations
    D-->>S: Data Response
    S-->>U: Complete Response
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Server Entry**: `server/src/index.ts` - Express server initialization and route configuration
- **Client Entry**: `client/src/app/layout.tsx` - Next.js root layout with global styling
- **NLP Service**: `nlp-service/main.py` - FastAPI service for sentiment analysis
- **Package Manager**: `package.json` - Monorepo orchestration and development scripts

### Core Modules

#### Authentication System
```mermaid
classDiagram
    class AuthController {
        +register()
        +login()
        +getMe()
    }
    
    class AuthService {
        +registerUser()
        +loginUser()
        +getUserById()
    }
    
    class PasswordUtils {
        +hashPassword()
        +comparePassword()
    }
    
    class TokenUtils {
        +generateToken()
    }
    
    AuthController --> AuthService
    AuthService --> PasswordUtils
    AuthService --> TokenUtils
```

#### Data Models
The platform uses Prisma ORM with comprehensive data modeling:

```mermaid
erDiagram
    USER ||--o{ CONVERSATION : has
    USER ||--o{ MOOD_ENTRY : tracks
    USER ||--o{ PHQ9_ASSESSMENT : completes
    USER ||--o{ RISK_ALERT : triggers
    USER ||--o{ RECOMMENDATION : receives
    
    CONVERSATION ||--o{ MESSAGE : contains
    PHQ9_ASSESSMENT ||--o{ RISK_ALERT : generates
```

#### NLP Processing Pipeline
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Raw Text Input] --> B[Text Preprocessing]
    B --> C[NLTK Tokenization]
    C --> D[VADER Sentiment Analysis]
    D --> E[Sentiment Classification]
    E --> F[Compound Score Calculation]
    F --> G[Structured Response]
```

### Configuration Approach
- **Environment Variables**: Centralized configuration through `.env` files
- **TypeScript Configuration**: Strict typing across all components
- **Prisma Schema**: Database-first development with migration support
- **Docker Compose**: Containerized database deployment

### External Dependencies

#### Backend Dependencies (`server/package.json`)
- **Express**: Web application framework
- **Prisma**: Type-safe database ORM
- **JWT**: Authentication tokens
- **Bcrypt**: Password hashing
- **CORS**: Cross-origin resource sharing

#### Frontend Dependencies (`client/package.json`)
- **Next.js**: React framework with SSR
- **React**: UI library
- **Tailwind CSS**: Utility-first styling
- **TypeScript**: Type safety

#### NLP Dependencies (`nlp-service/requirements.txt`)
- **FastAPI**: High-performance Python web framework
- **NLTK**: Natural language processing toolkit
- **VADER**: Lexicon-based sentiment analysis
- **Pydantic**: Data validation and settings management

### Integration Points
- **Database Integration**: PostgreSQL with Prisma ORM
- **Authentication Flow**: JWT-based session management
- **NLP Integration**: RESTful API calls between services
- **File Uploads**: Future enhancement for document analysis

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

#### Student Features
- **AI Chat Assistant**: Intelligent conversation support with sentiment analysis
- **Mental Health Assessments**: Automated PHQ-9 depression screening
- **Mood Tracking**: Daily mood logging with trend visualization
- **Personalized Recommendations**: AI-generated coping strategies
- **Secure Messaging**: Encrypted communication with counselors

#### Counselor Features
- **Alert Management**: Real-time risk detection and alert review
- **Student Dashboard**: Comprehensive student progress monitoring
- **Case Management**: Organized student record keeping
- **Communication Tools**: Secure messaging with students

### Implementation Highlights

#### Authentication Flow
```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Unauthenticated
    Unauthenticated --> Login: User attempts login
    Login --> Authenticated: Valid credentials
    Login --> Unauthenticated: Invalid credentials
    Authenticated --> Dashboard: Student view
    Authenticated --> CounsellorDashboard: Counselor view
    Dashboard --> [*]: Logout
    CounsellorDashboard --> [*]: Logout
```

#### Assessment Workflow
```mermaid
flowchart LR
    A[Student Access] --> B[PHQ-9 Questionnaire]
    B --> C[AI Analysis]
    C --> D[Risk Scoring]
    D --> E[Recommendations]
    E --> F[Alert Generation]
    F --> G[Counselor Notification]
```

#### Data Flow Architecture
```mermaid
graph LR
    A[User Input] --> B[Frontend Validation]
    B --> C[API Gateway]
    C --> D[Authentication Middleware]
    D --> E[Business Logic]
    E --> F[Database Operations]
    F --> G[Response Formatting]
    G --> H[Frontend Display]
```

The BuddyAI platform represents a sophisticated solution for mental health support, combining modern web technologies with advanced AI capabilities to provide accessible, scalable, and effective mental healthcare services for educational institutions.

---

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [server/src/index.ts](server/src/index.ts)
- [client/src/app/layout.tsx](client/src/app/layout.tsx)
- [prisma/schema.prisma](prisma/schema.prisma)
- [nlp-service/main.py](nlp-service/main.py)
- [server/src/config/env.ts](server/src/config/env.ts)
- [client/src/components/Navbar.tsx](client/src/components/Navbar.tsx)
- [server/src/controllers/auth.controller.ts](server/src/controllers/auth.controller.ts)
- [server/src/services/auth.service.ts](server/src/services/auth.service.ts)
- [nlp-service/models.py](nlp-service/models.py)
- [client/src/app/page.tsx](client/src/app/page.tsx)
- [docker-compose.yml](docker-compose.yml)

</blog>
